question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Math.min(limit, listenerProperties.getMaxPageSize()) calculation in poll() to always receive up to maxPageSize (5000) messages per poll cycle from a topic they do not own, since no ownership check gates the page size computation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the .delaySubscription(interval, scheduler) in listen() to confirm that the subscription is accepted and queued before any authorization check could theoretically occur, meaning the system has already committed to serving the unauthorized subscription before any validation window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the boundedElastic scheduler in listen() to open many concurrent unauthorized subscriptions to different topics, consuming thread pool resources and receiving messages from all topics without any signer or owner approval for any of them?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that poll() uses Flux.fromStream(topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter)) with no row-level security or ownership predicate, meaning the database query returns all messages for the given topicId regardless of the caller's identity or signing authority?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of any gRPC interceptor or middleware authorization check visible in PollingTopicListener.listen() to subscribe to any topic, since the listener itself is the last line of defense and it performs no signer/owner validation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user supply a TopicMessageFilter with a subscriberId they control (overriding the random default) to correlate their subscription across multiple listen() calls, and because no ownership check ties subscriberId to an authorized identity, maintain persistent unauthorized access to a topic's transaction stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the newFilter built in poll() via filter.toBuilder() inherits the original topicId without any re-authorization, meaning even if the topic's admin key is rotated after subscription, the polling continues to deliver messages without the new owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the RepeatSpec jitter(0.1) in listen() to slightly randomize polling intervals, making their unauthorized subscription harder to detect via rate-based anomaly detection, while continuously receiving topic messages without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Micrometer observation tap in listen() to infer, via exposed metrics (hiero_mirror_grpc_listener with tag mode=poll), that their unauthorized subscription is active and delivering messages, confirming successful unauthorized access without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that TopicMessageFilter.subscriberId is generated with RandomStringUtils.random() and never validated against an authenticated session, meaning any caller can construct a filter with any subscriberId and receive topic messages without the topic owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the context.getCount().get() usage in poll() to confirm message delivery counts from a topic they do not own, using the count as a side-channel oracle to determine topic activity levels without the owner's approval or knowledge?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a topic existence check combined with no ownership validation in listen(), such that they can probe arbitrary EntityId values as topicIds and, for any that return messages, confirm the topic exists and read its full transaction history without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that listen() accepts a TopicMessageFilter with no cryptographic proof of identity (no signature, no key, no token), meaning the entire authorization model for topic message access is absent, allowing any caller to authorize their own access without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the sequential consensus timestamp advancement in poll() (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1) to reconstruct the exact ordering and timing of all transactions submitted to a topic they do not own, enabling transaction replay analysis without the topic owner's approval?
